name: Sodium Benzoate
description: Common preservative linked to hyperactivity when combined with artificial colors.

name: Potassium Sorbate
description: Widely used preservative, generally recognized as safe.

name: Sodium Nitrite
description: Used in processed meats, linked to carcinogenic nitrosamines.

name: BHA
description: Butylated hydroxyanisole — potential endocrine disruptor.

name: BHT
description: Butylated hydroxytoluene — synthetic antioxidant.

name: Yellow 5
description: Yellow azo dye linked to hyperactivity in children.

name: Red 40
description: Most widely used food dye, linked to behavioral issues.

name: Yellow 6
description: Synthetic azo dye, banned in some countries.

name: Blue 1
description: Petroleum-derived synthetic dye.

name: Caramel Color
description: Some types contain 4-MEI, a potential carcinogen.

name: Monosodium Glutamate
description: Can cause sensitivity reactions in some individuals.

name: Aspartame
description: IARC classified as possibly carcinogenic to humans.

name: Sucralose
description: Non-caloric sweetener, 600x sweeter than sugar.

name: Acesulfame K
description: Limited long-term studies available.

name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
description: Linked to obesity, diabetes, and metabolic syndrome.

name: Carrageenan
description: Seaweed-derived, linked to gut inflammation.

name: Polysorbate 80
description: Synthetic emulsifier, may affect gut microbiome.

name: Soy Lecithin
description: Natural emulsifier derived from soybeans.

name: Xanthan Gum
description: Fermented polysaccharide used as thickener.

name: Guar Gum
description: Natural fiber from guar beans.

name: TBHQ
description: Synthetic preservative, may cause DNA damage.

name: Sulfites
description: Can trigger asthma attacks in sensitive individuals.

name: Citric Acid
description: Natural acid found in citrus fruits, used as preservative.

name: Ascorbic Acid
description: Vitamin C, natural antioxidant and preservative.

name: Calcium Propionate
description: Mold inhibitor commonly used in bread.

name: Sodium Nitrate
description: Converts to nitrite, linked to cancer risk.

name: Titanium Dioxide
description: White pigment, banned in EU due to health concerns.

name: Blue 2
description: Synthetic dye used in beverages and candies.

name: Red 3
description: Cherry-red dye, restricted in some countries.

name: Annatto
description: Natural orange-red color from achiote seeds.

name: Beta Carotene
description: Natural orange pigment, vitamin A precursor.

name: Turmeric
description: Natural yellow spice with anti-inflammatory properties.

name: Sodium Stearoyl Lactylate
description: Dough conditioner and emulsifier.

name: DATEM
description: Diacetyl tartaric acid ester, dough strengthener.

name: Gelatin
description: Protein derived from collagen, used as gelling agent.

name: Pectin
description: Natural fiber from fruits, used as thickener.

name: Agar
description: Seaweed-derived gelling agent.

name: Cellulose Gum
description: Carboxymethyl cellulose, plant-based thickener.

name: Yeast Extract
description: Natural flavor enhancer rich in glutamates.

name: Disodium Inosinate
description: Flavor enhancer often used with MSG.

name: Disodium Guanylate
description: Flavor enhancer that amplifies umami taste.

name: Corn Syrup
description: Glucose syrup derived from corn starch.

name: Stevia
description: Natural zero-calorie sweetener from stevia plant.

name: Erythritol
description: Sugar alcohol with minimal calories.

name: Sorbitol
description: Sugar alcohol, may cause digestive issues in large amounts.

name: Maltitol
description: Sugar alcohol used in sugar-free products.

name: Sorbic Acid
description: Natural antimicrobial preservative.

name: Natamycin
description: Natural antifungal used on cheese surfaces.

name: Nisin
description: Natural antimicrobial peptide from bacteria.

name: Sodium Propionate
description: Mold inhibitor used in baked goods.

name: Potassium Benzoate
description: Preservative similar to sodium benzoate.

name: Tocopherols
description: Vitamin E, natural antioxidant.

name: Sodium Erythorbate
description: Antioxidant used in cured meats.
